issue_title: crypto.com historic ohlcv data (since parameter) ineffective
issue_comments: @xmatthias Thanks for the report. We'll fix this asap.

I think the fix results in the input argument requested timestamp not being included in the result.

```
$ npm run cli.js cryptocom fetchOHLCV BTC/USDT 5m 1708128000000 1

> ccxt@4.3.60 cli.js
> node ./examples/js/cli.js cryptocom fetchOHLCV BTC/USDT 5m 1708128000000 1

2024-07-12T18:39:52.416Z
Node.js: v22.4.1
CCXT v4.3.60
cryptocom.fetchOHLCV (BTC/USDT, 5m, 1708128000000, 1)
2024-07-12T18:39:53.100Z iteration 0 passed in 230 ms

1708128300000 | 52116.72 | 52142.99 | 52077.63 | 52106.99 | 14.37064
1 objects
2024-07-12T18:39:53.100Z iteration 1 passed in 230 ms
```

Here the input argument is `1708128000000` but the output is `1708128300000` which is `1708128000000 + 5*60*1000`.

Also, would you prefer comments like this on the original bug or the issue with the actual PR?

uppon testing this, i noticed the same

@sc0Vu maybe you could take a look again?

@blair Thanks, they didn't mention the max of limit is 300. BTW, I think it wouldn't work if set `start_ts` to `since`. It seems they added duration in the server.

Test (the request of `start_t` is `since`):
```BASH
$ p cryptocom fetchOHLCV BTC/USDT 5m 1708128000000 1 => 1708128300000
$ p cryptocom fetchOHLCV BTC/USDT 5m 1708127900000 1 => 1708128300000
$ p cryptocom fetchOHLCV BTC/USDT 5m 1708127800000 1 => 1708128300000
$ p cryptocom fetchOHLCV BTC/USDT 5m 1708127700000 1 => 1708128000000
$ p cryptocom fetchOHLCV BTC/USDT 5m 1708127600000 1 => 1708128000000
$ p cryptocom fetchOHLCV BTC/USDT 5m 1708127500000 1 => 1708128000000
$ p cryptocom fetchOHLCV BTC/USDT 5m 1708127400000 1 => 1708127700000
```

Anyway, will fix this asap.

issue_title: fetch balance doesn't work on bybit inverse account
issue_body:
### Operating System

linux

### Programming Languages

Python

### CCXT Version

4.3.55

### Description

fetch_balance() always loads 'unified' wallet's balance. On bybit there is a unified (v5) API, but the inverse pairs have their own wallet, you can query the unified balance with `request['accountType'] = 'UNIFIED'` and the inverse balance with  `request['accountType'] = 'CONTRACT'`. In the latest ccxt code it doesn't work though because the branch which could set type to contract is unreachable if the account is unified.

```
        # from ccxt's bybit.py
        if isUnifiedAccount:
            if isSpot or isSwap:
                type = 'unified'
        else:
            if isSwap:
                type = 'contract'
```

fetch_open_orders() and fetch_positions() handles this properly.

for a quick workaround I can use
`exchange.parse_balance(exchange.privateGetV5AccountWalletBalance({'accountType': 'CONTRACT'}))`

### Code

```
  

```
issue_comments: @materemias We already did that https://github.com/ccxt/ccxt/blob/master/python/ccxt/bybit.py#L2924. You can set the type to swap `fetch_balance({'type': 'swap'})`.

@sc0Vu thanks for the prompt reply, I think it won't solve it. `isSwap` will be `True` (it gets picked up correctly anyways) but since `isUnifiedAccount` is also `True` we never get to set `contract` as type.

![image](https://github.com/ccxt/ccxt/assets/529994/6eee1731-153c-4b8b-9081-99542c1d4c9b)
`fetch_balance({'type': 'contract'})` will work though, thanks for the hint! (still the autodetection logic is broken)
@materemias I think you're right. The type should be contract for unified account (my test account was non-unified). We'll fix this asap.

issue_title: Kucoin watchMyTrades uses undocumented API
issue_body:
### Operating System

Debian 12

### Programming Languages

Python

### CCXT Version

4.3.31

### Description

I am using CCXT Pro to access Kucoin. I am noticing that the `watch_my_trades()` function is not giving me any messages even when fills are happening on account. Looking under the covers, it seems that CCXT is subscribing to the `/spot/tradeFills` topic. I am looking at Kucoin websocket API documentation and I do not see any trade or fills topic. The closest I see is a "Private Order Change" topic that uses `/spotMarket/` instead of `/spot/`.

I am wondering whether is CCXT relying on an undocumented API endpoint? Or am I missing something? Could anyone else using Kucoin verify whether their `watch_my_trades()` works or not?

Some relevant links:

Code in CCXT subscribing to the `/spot/tradeFills` topic: https://github.com/ccxt/ccxt/blob/master/python/ccxt/pro/kucoin.py#L909

Private Order Change topic: https://www.kucoin.com/docs/websocket/spot-trading/private-channels/private-order-change

### Code

```
  

```
issue_comments: @colin-nl it's probably a deprecated topic. We'll update asap.

        paginationCursor = self.safe_string_2(result, 'nextPageCursor', 'cursor')
        dataLength = len(data)
        if (paginationCursor is not None) and (dataLength > 0):
            first = data[0]
            first['nextPageCursor'] = paginationCursor
            data[0] = first
        return data
```

  File "./venv/lib/python3.8/site-packages/ccxt/base/exchange.py", line 695, in fetch

The included order which leads to the exception is:

issue_title: `handle_errors()` causes Critical Error while using a wrong types for `$http_status_text` and `$result` params
issue_body:
### Operating System

Any

### Programming Languages

PHP

### CCXT Version

4.2+

### Description

In `php/Exchange.php` file in `fetch()` method you have a big mistake of using nulls for params `$http_status_text` and `$result ? $result : null` during the call of `$this->handle_errors()`. Because this method for all exchanges expects to have `string` as a type for this 2 params. And sometimes they can be null which causes a php Critical Error.

### Code

Currently:

```
$skip_further_error_handling = $this->handle_errors($http_status_code, $http_status_text, $url, $method, $response_headers, $result ? $result : null, $json_response, $headers, $body);  
```

Should be:

```
$skip_further_error_handling = $this->handle_errors($http_status_code, (string) $http_status_text, $url, $method, $response_headers, $result ? $result : '', $json_response, $headers, $body);  
```

Or you can use `''` directly for `$http_status_text` instead of using `null`:

```
$http_status_text = count($parts) === 3 ? $parts[2] : '';
```
issue_comments: Hi @AnakinA1, I think we can change the default value to empty string ''. Also, could I ask how to get the null situation? The api supposed to send correct response (`HTTP/1.1 STATUS STATUSTEXT`).
Hi, for example using `bitget` library it's very often situation when I have `null` in $http_status_text

issue_title: BitMEX - watch_order_book error once a day - safe_string default value NoneType and operation of return is to append a string
issue_body:
### Operating System

Ubuntu 20.04

### Programming Languages

Python

### CCXT Version

4.3.6

### Description

We are running a long-lived websocket connection for fetching the orderbook prices for XBTUSD on BitMEX. At least once a day, like clockwork, we get an exception that is born out of the guts of ccxt (it happens at around 2AM UTC every day, rarely it can also happen other times).

I assume the reason it happens on schedule is a BitMEX side thing - perhaps they are reseting all WS subscriptions on a clock. CCXT should have the internals for handling this. The issue seems to be that the return of the "safe_string" default is NoneType (when no alternative provided), but the operation on the return of this for 'table' is to append a string to it, which causes the exception below. I assume simply speciyfying a default_value == '' will suffice to avoid this exception, but I don't know what knock-on effects that may have down the line here so probably best to check here first.

### Stack Trace:
```
 File "/home/ubuntu/env/lib/python3.10/site-packages/ccxt/pro/bitmex.py", line 1121, in watch_order_book
    return await self.watch_order_book_for_symbols([symbol], limit, params)
  File "/home/ubuntu/env/lib/python3.10/site-packages/ccxt/pro/bitmex.py", line 1156, in watch_order_book_for_symbols
    orderbook = await self.watch_multiple(url, messageHashes, self.deep_extend(request, params), topics)
  File "/home/ubuntu/env/lib/python3.10/site-packages/ccxt/async_support/base/ws/fast_client.py", line 26, in handler
    self.handle_message(message)
  File "/home/ubuntu/env/lib/python3.10/site-packages/ccxt/async_support/base/ws/aiohttp_client.py", line 34, in handle_message
    self.handle_text_or_binary_message(message.data)
  File "/home/ubuntu/env/lib/python3.10/site-packages/ccxt/async_support/base/ws/aiohttp_client.py", line 29, in handle_text_or_binary_message
    self.on_message_callback(self, decoded)
  File "/home/ubuntu/env/lib/python3.10/site-packages/ccxt/pro/bitmex.py", line 1553, in handle_message
    method(client, message)
  File "/home/ubuntu/env/lib/python3.10/site-packages/ccxt/pro/bitmex.py", line 1402, in handle_order_book
    messageHash = table + ':' + symbol
TypeError: can only concatenate str (not "NoneType") to str
``` 

### Code I assume needs to be changed (?)

Current:
```
def handle_order_book(self, client: Client, message):
   (...)
   table = self.safe_string(message, 'table')
   (...)
```

To:
```
def handle_order_book(self, client: Client, message):
   (...)
   table = self.safe_string(message, 'table', '')
   (...)
```

### Code

```
  

```
issue_comments:
Hi @ryanmccoy, thanks, it looks like the symbol is `None` (the filter could be empty dict). I'll run it and see what the message is.

Which limit did you use?
@sc0Vu I don't provide a limit - to reiterate so that it is hopefully easier to replicate, like clockwork this can be observed at the same time every day: 2AM UTC

issue_title: Bingx - parse order price / cost wrong
issue_body:
### Operating System

python

### Programming Languages

Python

### CCXT Version

4.3.28

### Description

For (filled) stop orders - average is missing (probably a duplicate of #21419).

What's worse though is that cost is NOT correct.

This order filled at 0.0000094949 - and was for 10_417_100 - a USDT cost of `98.9103370717`.

The (parsed) result from ccxt however has "98.80306837" as cost - which (i assume) is simply price * amount.
The "stoploss" response from the exchange does have a corresponding field in `"cummulativeQuoteQty"` - which does contain the correct cost - and which i think should be used by ccxt.

I understand that `price` is the limit of the stop limit price - and i also kinda understand that average is not filled currently - as it's not provided by the exchange.

However unfortunately, the absence of average - combined with the wrongness of cost makes it currently near impossible to correctly calculate the price for an order (without using the info object) - which i find rather unfortunate.

```

issue_comments:
@xmatthias looks like the cost was wrong, what's the price of the order? I used `cummulativeQuoteQty / executedQty (9.494997366999999e-6)` or `(cummulativeQuoteQty - fee) / executedQty (9.485502369632964e-6)`, but the number didn't match......
hi @sc0Vu,

Well the price is shown in the screenshot (took a highlighted screenshot from the order below for you to highlight this).
the price shown on exchange IS 0.0000094949 - which is what your first calculation yielded `cummulativeQuoteQty / executedQty`.
you got `0.00000949497366999999` - which is the same for the first 10(!) decimals - and the rest, I'd assume to be a rounding problem (it'll most likely disappear if you do string math).

Now the exchange will truncate the remaining decimals - but it's a LOT better than what ccxt is currently providing - which is using the price of the limit order - so "stop price - 1%" in my case - which is 1% below the actual execution price.

![Screenshot](https://github.com/ccxt/ccxt/assets/5024695/80c7233a-322f-442e-856c-74e699e3522f)

issue_title: Kraken active flag giving false postive
issue_body:
### Operating System

macOS Sonoma 14.2.1

### Programming Languages

JavaScript

### CCXT Version

4.3.11

### Description

Some trading pairs have the `active` flag set to true for the Kraken exchange, even though they are in `cancel_only` mode on Kraken, meaning the only thing you can do is cancel existing trades. Actually that's not even true anymore since April 5, see [this](https://support.kraken.com/hc/en-us/articles/notice-of-spot-trading-pairs-removal).

Also according to the [public tradeable assets API](https://api.kraken.com/0/public/AssetPairs) multiple trading pairs, i.e. ZEC/BTC are 'cancel_only', so not sure why the active flag is still true for them.

### Code

```
const kraken = new ccxt.pro.kraken()
console.log(kraken.markets['ZEC/BTC'].active) // true
```
issue_comments: Hello @mayrsascha, thanks for reporting it. You're right in that case `active` should be false, we will fix it shortly.

issue_title: python static method parse_date() incorrectly parse for some minutes or hour values with leading zeros
issue_body:
### Operating System

macos

### Programming Languages

Python

### CCXT Version

4.2.100

### Description

The parse_date static method in exchange.py does not correctly handle minute values in the range 00 through 05. Similar problems are also seen with hour range 00-02
During the conversion process the minutes are converted to values and back to strings and joined. Minutes like 01 are converted to 1. The seconds values from the final concatenated string are than part of the minutes and not seconds.

Example
`exchange.parse_date('Mon, 29 Apr 2024 14:03:17 GMT')
`
Will become:
`1714401067000
`
However
`exchange.iso8601(1714401067000)` is ` '2024-04-29T14:31:07.000Z'`

More examples:
Bad:
```python
exchange.iso8601(exchange.parse_date('Mon, 29 Apr 2024 14:00:17 GMT'))
'2024-04-29T14:01:07.000Z'
```
Good:
```python
exchange.iso8601(exchange.parse_date('Mon, 29 Apr 2024 14:09:17 GMT'))
'2024-04-29T14:09:17.000Z'
```

Same problem seen when hours value is 01:
```python
exchange.iso8601(exchange.parse_date('Mon, 29 Dec 2024 01:01:10 GMT'))
'2024-12-29T11:01:00.000Z'
```

A potential solution is to use str.rjust(2, '0') and add '0' padding.
This solution was quickly tested locally:
OLD
```
In [97]: exchange.iso8601(exchange.parse_date('Mon, 29 Dec 2024 02:11:10 GMT'))
Out[97]: '2024-12-29T21:11:00.000Z'
```
NEW
```
In [15]: exchange.iso8601(exchange.parse_date('Mon, 29 Dec 2024 02:11:10 GMT'))
Out[15]: '2024-12-29T02:11:10.000Z'
```

### Code

```python
--- a/python/ccxt/base/exchange.py
+++ b/python/ccxt/base/exchange.py
@@ -1216,7 +1216,7 @@ class Exchange(object):
             return None
         if 'GMT' in timestamp:
             try:
-                string = ''.join([str(value) for value in parsedate(timestamp)[:6]]) + '.000Z'
+                string = ''.join([str(value).rjust(2, '0') for value in parsedate(timestamp)[:6]]) + '.000Z'
                 dt = datetime.datetime.strptime(string, "%Y%m%d%H%M%S.%fZ")
                 return calendar.timegm(dt.utctimetuple()) * 1000
             except (TypeError, OverflowError, OSError):
```
issue_comments: Hello @DelAnt, thanks a lot for reporting it; we will take a look and let you know as soon as possible.
I'll push a fix within a hour. thanks for report.

issue_title: Deribit: fetch_tickers returns an empty dict for non-BTC currencies
issue_body:
### Operating System

Linux, fedora 38

### Programming Languages

Python

### CCXT Version

4.2.96

### Description

On Deribit `fetch_tickers` works just for BTC, for other currencies it returns an empty dict although the pair is correctly inside `exchange.markets`.

### Code

```python
import ccxt
e = ccxt.deribit()
e.load_markets()
e.fetch_tickers(['ETH/USDT'])
# returns incorrectly an empty dict
e.fetch_tickers(['SOL/USDC'])
# returns incorrectly an empty dict
e.fetch_tickers(['BTC/USDT'])
# returns correctly {'BTC/USDT': ...}
"ETH/USDT" in e.markets
# returns True
"SOL/USDC" in e.markets
# returns True
```

issue_title: `define_rest_api` method missing cause error thrown when unserializing
issue_body:
### Operating System

Ubuntu

### Programming Languages

PHP

### CCXT Version

4.2.90

### Description

Hi there, many thanks to this great project.

The [define_rest_api](https://github.com/ccxt/ccxt/blob/6628f54c1c3abee7965d2ae2ac9aa5b408eb0f59/php/Exchange.php#L1703) method no longer exists, causing an error when the instance is unserialized. 

It appears we neglected to remove define_rest_api in this commit https://github.com/ccxt/ccxt/commit/30669190b87041f4bc7e77f2d769dfd80a694688

Also issue https://github.com/ccxt/ccxt/issues/18698 might be related to this bug.

### Code

```php
$obj = new \ccxt\htx();
unserialize(serialize($obj)); // will throw error:  ccxt\ExchangeError  define_rest_api method not found, try underscore_notation instead of camelCase for the method being called.

```
issue_comments: @Lorca-R thanks, will fix this asap.

issue_title: gemini: new '*' appended to market name in https://docs.gemini.com/rest-api/ needs to be trimmed
issue_body:
### Operating System

macOs

### Programming Languages

JavaScript

### CCXT Version

4.2.90

### Description

https://docs.gemini.com/rest-api/#symbols-and-minimums is used by `fetchMarketsFromWeb()` and it appears the webpage just added a `*` to some markets:

```
usdtusd*	0.1 USDT (1e-1)	0.000001 USDT (1e-6)	0.0001 USD (1e-4)
```

```
*Note: Not available to trade for customers in New York
```

This breaks `#fetchOHLCV` since it tries to get https://api.gemini.com/v2/candles/btcusd*/1m.

The fix appears to be in this code

```
    async fetchMarketsFromWeb (params = {}) {
...
            //     [
            //         '<td>btcusd', // currency
            //         '<td>0.00001 BTC (1e-5)', // min order size
            //         '<td>0.00000001 BTC (1e-8)', // tick size
            //         '<td>0.01 USD', // quote currency price increment
            //         '</tr>'
            //     ]
            const marketId = cells[0].replace ('<td>', '');
```

Adding another `.replace(/\*+$/, '')` to the `marketId`.

### Code

```
  

```
issue_comments: @blair thanks, will fix this asap.

issue_title: Kraken conflicting symbol 'REP/USD'
issue_body:
### Operating System

Linux

### Programming Languages

Python

### CCXT Version

4.2.89

### Description

On kraken, there exist two versions of the REP token: REP and REPV2. In older ccxt versions (4.2.47), the symbol REP/USD would map to REPV2/USD on kraken, and symbol REPV1/USD would map to REP/USD on kraken. However after updating, it seems that REPV1/USD symbol is no longer supported in ccxt, as both kraken markets REPV2/USD and REP/USD are now linked to ccxt symbol REP/USD. 

I am not sure how to access each market seperately now. 

### Code

```
import ccxt
kr = ccxt.kraken()
mkts = kr.load_markets()
# in mkts, only REP/USD is present. How do i access both REP/USD and REPV2/USD?

```
issue_comments: @Rickkert00 the issue is related to ccxt/ccxt#21112, with the patch merged the currency code would be changed if it's in the reverse order. Will fix this.

Recently there was merged a PR that fixed the issue for commonCurrencies. with regard to kraken, i am not fully sure where the problem lies at this moment, because when I looked into  currencies ( https://api.kraken.com/0/public/Assets ) or markets ( https://api.kraken.com/0/public/AssetPairs ), I nowhere see `REP` currency anymore, there are `REPV2` and `XREP` (which has 'altname' `REP` but altnames are not read &used in ccxt). So, there should not be a problem, as ccxt has such mapping in `commonCurrencies`:
```
'REPV2': 'REP',
```
so, when you'll want to interact with `REPV2/USD` market on kraken, with ccxt you'll be able to refer it with `REP/USD`.

and as mentioned above, there is also `XREP/USD` market. if you want to change that currency code, you can add smth like:
```
'XREP': 'REPV1'
```
so, you can then refer with `REPV1/USD` instead of `XREP/USD`
The only issue that is present now in kraken, is that it has now-obsolete `'REP': 'REPV1',` which I'll push to be removed

after the PR is merged and this issue autoclosed, you can update ccxt and it would have solved the problem. 
so, as seen in upcoming PR: https://github.com/ccxt/ccxt/pull/22067/files#diff-d577c74f7dbeddac30dac13c4097bf0aa9338629f0e3ae3a43677d980f80a24cR224

We will use this mapping:
```
                'REPV2': 'REP',
                'XREP': 'REPV1',
```
so, you'll be able to use `REP/USD` (which will be REPV2USD market) and `REPV1/USD` (which will be XREP/USD) market
wait, the problem seems deeper, this needs a while to fix the architecture.

issue_title: an endless loop after using fetch_leverage_tiers in mexc
issue_body:
### Operating System

Ubuntu 22.04

### Programming Languages

Python

### CCXT Version

4.1.1

### Description

hello
exchange=MEXC
When I used the “**fetch_leverage_tiers**”  in mexc.py, I encountered an endless loop and found that it was a problem in the “**parse_market_leverage_tiers**”. Specifically, when maxVol=5500 and riskIncrVol=0, the **while**  did not add any value to the “**floor**”, resulting in the **while**  not stopping,this is  a bug?

### Code

```python
      def parse_market_leverage_tiers(self, info, market=None):
        """
            @param info {object} Exchange response for 1 market
            @param market {object} CCXT market
        """
        maintenanceMarginRate = self.safe_string(info, 'maintenanceMarginRate')
        initialMarginRate = self.safe_string(info, 'initialMarginRate')
        maxVol = self.safe_string(info, 'maxVol')
        riskIncrVol = self.safe_string(info, 'riskIncrVol')
        riskIncrMmr = self.safe_string(info, 'riskIncrMmr')
        riskIncrImr = self.safe_string(info, 'riskIncrImr')
        floor = '0'
        tiers = []
        quoteId = self.safe_string(info, 'quoteCoin')
        while(Precise.string_lt(floor, maxVol)):
            cap = Precise.string_add(floor, riskIncrVol)
            tiers.append({
                'tier': self.parse_number(Precise.string_div(cap, riskIncrVol)),
                'currency': self.safe_currency_code(quoteId),
                'notionalFloor': self.parse_number(floor),
                'notionalCap': self.parse_number(cap),
                'maintenanceMarginRate': self.parse_number(maintenanceMarginRate),
                'maxLeverage': self.parse_number(Precise.string_div('1', initialMarginRate)),
                'info': info,
            })
            initialMarginRate = Precise.string_add(initialMarginRate, riskIncrImr)
            maintenanceMarginRate = Precise.string_add(maintenanceMarginRate, riskIncrMmr)
            floor = cap
        return tiers

issue_title: Kucoin: Invalid amount increment for createMarketOrderWithCost()
issue_body:
### Operating System

MacOS

### Programming Languages

JavaScript

### CCXT Version

4.2.80

### Description

When using createMarketOrderWithCost() on the Kucoin exchange with e.g. cost 0.89933212 on BTC/USDT, I get the error "The amount increment is invalid.". I went digging and it seems that ccxt is using the wrong increment property for "funds" (cost) on market orders.

In the ccxt code (->createOrderRequest), it uses amountToPrecision() for the funds param. This method always uses  "baseIncrement" as precision. That's on purpose as there's a comment that says "kucoin uses base precision even for quote values". However, the Kucoin documentation instructs to use the quoteIncrement for the funds parameter when placing market orders, see https://www.kucoin.com/docs/rest/spot-trading/orders/place-order. For BTC/USDT that's a difference between 0.00000001 and 0.000001, so the API rightfully throws an error.

I have a workaround working for now, but would be nice to have this fixed.

### Code

How to reproduce:
```javascript
const cost = 0.8993321164
const order = await exchange.createMarketOrderWithCost("BTC/USDT", "buy", cost);
// --> "Error: The amount increment is invalid."
```

My workaround:
```javascript
const cost = exchange.decimalToPrecision(
  originalCost,
  ccxt.functions.TRUNCATE,
  exchange.market("BTC/USDT").info.quoteIncrement,
  exchange.precisionMode,
  exchange.paddingMode
);

const order = await exchange.createMarketOrderWithCost("BTC/USDT", "buy", cost);
```
issue_comments: @mjwvb thanks, we'll fix this asap.

issue_title: [Hyperliquid] Bug on create_order: wrong format of the request
issue_body:
### Operating System

Windows 10

### Programming Languages

Python

### CCXT Version

4.2.74

### Description

The create_order() function does not work when any additional parameters are included via the 'params' dictionary. For example, this call:

exchange.create_order('BTC/USDT:USDT, 'limit', 'buy', 0.0005, 64000, params={'timeInForce': 'Gtc'})

Raises: 
ExchangeError('hyperliquid POST https://api.hyperliquid.xyz/exchange 422 Unprocessable Entity Failed to deserialize the JSON body into the target type')

The reason is that the create_orders() function uses "extend(request, params)" to add the raw parameters passed by the user to the request, which causes the request body to not conform to the API definition ( https://hyperliquid.gitbook.io/hyperliquid-docs/for-developers/api/exchange-endpoint).

I have solved the problem locally by simply changing "extend(request, params)" to "request".

### Code

```
  # Request sent:
{
    "action": {
        "type": "order",
        "orders": [
            {
                "a": 0,
                "b": true,
                "p": "63874",
                "s": "0.00053",
                "r": false,
                "t": {
                    "limit": {
                        "tif": "Gtc"
                    }
                }
            }
        ],
        "grouping": "na",
        "brokerCode": 1
    },
    "nonce": 1710883653272,
    "signature": {
        "r": "0x...",
        "s": "0x...",
        "v": 28
    },
    "timeInForce": "Gtc" # <- THIS SHOULD NOT BE HERE
}

async def create_orders(self, orders: List[OrderRequest], params={}):
    ...
    ...
    ...
    request = {
        'action': orderAction,
        'nonce': nonce,
        'signature': signature,
        # 'vaultAddress': vaultAddress,
    }
    response = await self.privatePostExchange(self.extend(request, params)) # <- Request should not be extendend with params
    # Replacing with this line makes it work: 
    # response = await self.privatePostExchange(request)
```
issue_comments: @GeminonProtocol thanks, will fix this asap.

@GeminonProtocol The newly released version fixes the issue so you can update if you want.

Thanks once again for reporting it!

https://docs.cloud.coinbase.com/advanced-trade-api/reference/retailbrokerageapi_getaccounts

however the implementation here using `self.load_accounts()` is not taking a paginated param into account and this is searching for a code in an incomplete data set.

```
    def find_account_id(self, code):
        self.load_markets()
        self.load_accounts()
        for i in range(0, len(self.accounts)):
            account = self.accounts[i]
            if account['code'] == code:
                return account['id']
        return None
```

Example code:

deposits = exchange.fetch_deposits(code="GBP", since=since, params={"paginate": True})
```
above code results in

```
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.11/site-packages/ccxt/coinbase.py", line 737, in fetch_deposits
    return self.fetch_transactions_with_method('v2PrivateGetAccountsAccountIdDeposits', code, since, limit, params)
           ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.11/site-packages/ccxt/coinbase.py", line 708, in fetch_transactions_with_method
    request, params = self.prepare_account_request_with_currency_code(code, limit, params)
                      ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^

    raise ExchangeError(self.id + ' prepareAccountRequestWithCurrencyCode() could not find account id for ' + code)
ccxt.base.errors.ExchangeError: coinbase prepareAccountRequestWithCurrencyCode() could not find account id for GBP

calling `exchange.fetch_accounts_v3(params={"paginate": True})`  seems to return all accounts 

### Code

```
  

```
issue_comments:
as a workaround I have to implement something like:

```
def custom_find_account_id(code):
  accounts = exchange.fetch_accounts_v3(params={"paginate": True})
  for i in range(0, len(accounts)):
    account = accounts[i]
    if account['code'] == code:
      return account['id']
  return None
```

```
code = 'GBP'

```

issue_title: Bybit leverage tiers parsing
issue_body:
### Operating System

_No response_

### Programming Languages

Python

### CCXT Version

4.2.76

### Description

Based on the ccxt docs, leverage tiers should include minNotional and maxNotional.

```
{
        "tier": 1,                       // tier index
        "notionalCurrency": "USDT",      // the currency that minNotional and maxNotional are in
        "minNotional": 0,                // the lowest amount of this tier // stake = 0.0
        "maxNotional": 10000,            // the highest amount of this tier // max stake amount at 75x leverage = 133.33333333333334
        "maintenanceMarginRate": 0.0065, // maintenance margin rate
        "maxLeverage": 75,               // max available leverage for this market when the value of the trade is > minNotional and < maxNotional
        "info": { ... }                  // Response from exchange
    },
```

https://docs.ccxt.com/#/README?id=leverage-tiers


The new bybit "fetch_leverage_tiers()` is lacking parsing for this, returning for example this:

```
[{'tier': 1,
  'currency': None,
  'minNotional': None,
  'maxNotional': None,
  'maintenanceMarginRate': 0.005,
  'maxLeverage': 100.0,
  'info': {'id': '1',
   'symbol': 'BTCUSDT',
   'riskLimitValue': '2000000',
   'maintenanceMargin': '0.005',
   'initialMargin': '0.01',
   'isLowestRisk': '1',
   'maxLeverage': '100.00'}},
 {'tier': 2,
  'currency': None,
  'minNotional': None,
  'maxNotional': None,
  'maintenanceMarginRate': 0.0056,
  'maxLeverage': 90.0,
  'info': {'id': '2',
   'symbol': 'BTCUSDT',
   'riskLimitValue': '2600000',
   'maintenanceMargin': '0.0056',
   'initialMargin': '0.0111',
   'isLowestRisk': '0',
   'maxLeverage': '90.00'}},
// ...
```

The information to properly parse both currency (symbol - but in CCXT terms) - as well as the min/max notional - which in my understanding, should be comming from `riskLimitValue` (which delimits `maxNotional` - with minNotional being filled with the value from the previous tier, or 0 in case of the initial tier.

The way this endpoint looks currently, it's not usable (not least because it's even missing the currency).

### Code

``` python
import ccxt
exchange = ccxt.bybit({
    'options': {'defaultType': 'swap'} 
    })
lm = exchange.load_markets()

lt = exchange.fetch_leverage_tiers()
print(lt['BTC/USDT:USDT'])

``` 

Now unfortunately - the change to the parsing also broke the use of `exchange.fetch_market_leverage_tiers('BTC/USDT:USDT')` - which was working perfectly fine in prior versions  (e.g. 4.2.67 - essentially anything prior to #21747), and returned the following:

```
[{'tier': 1,
  'currency': 'BTC',
  'minNotional': 0,
  'maxNotional': 2000000.0,
  'maintenanceMarginRate': 0.005,
  'maxLeverage': 100.0,
  'info': {'id': '1',
   'symbol': 'BTCUSDT',
   'riskLimitValue': '2000000',
   'maintenanceMargin': '0.005',
   'initialMargin': '0.01',
   'isLowestRisk': '1',
   'maxLeverage': '100.00'}},
 {'tier': 2,
  'currency': 'BTC',
  'minNotional': 2000000.0,
  'maxNotional': 2600000.0,
  'maintenanceMarginRate': 0.0056,
  'maxLeverage': 90.0,
  'info': {'id': '2',
   'symbol': 'BTCUSDT',
   'riskLimitValue': '2600000',
   'maintenanceMargin': '0.0056',
   'initialMargin': '0.0111',
   'isLowestRisk': '0',
   'maxLeverage': '90.00'}},
//...
]
```
issue_comments:
@xmatthias thanks, will add maxNotional asap.
@carlosmiei for the minNotional, I'm not sure whether we should use the previous notional value (need to find the previous leverage tier with the same symbol).

@sc0Vu that's how it worked previously, too - don't see why you should remove that behavior, really.

So to be clear, my expectation is that both minNotional and maxNotional are there - otherwise it's a regression from previous versions.
@xmatthias Thanks we will fill those values as suggested.

issue_title: Binance / Hyperliquid: Timestamp inconsistency in fetch_ohlcv
issue_body:
### Operating System

Windows 10

### Programming Languages

Python

### CCXT Version

4.2.74

### Description

The timestamp values returned by the fetch_ohlcv() function are inconsistent between exchanges.

For Hyperliquid, the function returns the closing timestamp of the candle, while for Binance it returns the opening timestamp. Most exchanges return the candle opening time. This inconsistency breaks the exchange interoperability that this library intends, I recommend changing the hyperliquid code so that it returns the opening timestamp as well.

Additionally, in the Binance code the timestamp is labeled as the closing of the candle when in reality the parser returns the opening time.

### Code

```
    # BINANCE
  def parse_ohlcv(self, ohlcv, market: Market = None) -> list:
        # when api method = publicGetKlines or fapiPublicGetKlines or dapiPublicGetKlines
        #     [
        #         1591478520000,  # open time
        #         "0.02501300",  # open
        #         "0.02501800",  # high
        #         "0.02500000",  # low
        #         "0.02500000",  # close
        #         "22.19000000",  # volume
        #         1591478579999,  # close time
        #         "0.55490906",  # quote asset volume, base asset volume for dapi
        #         40,            # number of trades
        #         "10.92900000",  # taker buy base asset volume
        #         "0.27336462",  # taker buy quote asset volume
        #         "0"            # ignore
        #     ]
        volumeIndex = 7 if inverse else 5
        return [
            self.safe_integer_2(ohlcv, 0, 'closeTime'), # <- This is openTime, not closeTime
            self.safe_number_2(ohlcv, 1, 'open'),
            self.safe_number_2(ohlcv, 2, 'high'),
            self.safe_number_2(ohlcv, 3, 'low'),
            self.safe_number_2(ohlcv, 4, 'close'),
            self.safe_number_2(ohlcv, volumeIndex, 'volume'),
        ]

    # HYPERLIQUID
    def parse_ohlcv(self, ohlcv, market: Market = None) -> list:
        #
        #     {
        #         "T": 1704287699999,
        #         "c": "2226.4",
        #         "h": "2247.9",
        #         "i": "15m",
        #         "l": "2224.6",
        #         "n": 46,
        #         "o": "2247.9",
        #         "s": "ETH",
        #         "t": 1704286800000,
        #         "v": "591.6427"
        #     }
        #
        return [
            self.safe_integer(ohlcv, 'T'), # <- This should be 't' (open time), not 'T' (close time) for consistency
            self.safe_number(ohlcv, 'o'),
            self.safe_number(ohlcv, 'h'),
            self.safe_number(ohlcv, 'l'),
            self.safe_number(ohlcv, 'c'),
            self.safe_number(ohlcv, 'v'),
        ]
```
issue_comments: Hello @GeminonProtocol, thanks for bringing our attention to this. We will try to find a solution.

@GeminonProtocol Regarding your suggestion, I'm not sure if 't' represents the opening of the candle (assuming that for instance the daily candle opens at 00:00 but the first trade might be at 00:05) but not sure if this is a problem with the wording in the docs.
![image](https://github.com/ccxt/ccxt/assets/43336371/5b25b0d8-b6ef-435c-b3a1-8eaa7646e75b)
@GeminonProtocol We just confirmed and `t` is indeed the correct value, we will merge it shortly.
@GeminonProtocol If you update your ccxt version, this issue should be fixed 🙂

Thank you very much and great job guys!

(I already patched the library code locally, and also downloaded all the symbols to verify that the timestamps diff using 't' was a constant)

issue_title: Gate resolving wrong market symbol for spot stop orders
issue_body:
### Operating System

linux

### Programming Languages

Python

### CCXT Version

4.2.67

### Description

Parsing of stop orders seems to fall back to the wrong order symbol (futures instead of spot).

This results in a request to get a stop order for METIS/USDT to return an order for symbol METIS/USDT:USDT.
the remainder of the content seems correct - though "symbol" is not.
Now it's not something we directly check on - but definitely something that could be improved.

Interestingly, doing the same call for a non-stop order (obviously without params parameter and a changed ID) does parse the symbol just fine.


### Code

``` python
import ccxt
exchange = ccxt.gate({
    'apiKey': '<yourApiKey>', 
    'secret': '<yoursecret>'
    })
res = exchange.fetch_order( '683672990', 'METIS/USDT', params={'stop': True})
print(res)

issue_title: wrong position parsing with watchPositions
issue_body:
### Operating System

_No response_

### Programming Languages

_No response_

### CCXT Version

_No response_

### Description

https://github.com/ccxt/ccxt/blob/139e8ae040820a16a8f2e776c45c427fad4b702c/ts/src/pro/binance.ts#L2622

There are at least 2 issues here:
- `side` is set to `positionSide` which the endpoint returns _both_ when position is one way, not long or short
- `contracts` is set from the `pa` field. This field is negative for short positions but in ccxt the value should be always >= 0  afaik

so the sign of the `contracts` value should be fixed and use the sign to infer the position side

### Code

_No response_
issue_comments:
Hi @untoreh

I think negative contracts is an issue, will fix that asap. For the position side, I found the message would be zero when liquidate the position. In that condition, we need to loop the positions cache.....

issue_title: Mexc support is broken for python with the error: Invalid content Type
issue_body:
### Operating System

Cross platform

### Programming Languages

Python

### CCXT Version

Latest

### Description

Mexc recently released an update that breaks ccxt.

This is the update details: 
https://www.mexc.com/support/articles/17827791513387


The error I am getting now looks like this:
```
try:
  trades = await asyncio.wait_for(a.exchange.watch_my_trades(a.m.symbol, a.trade_ts), timeout=1800)
except asyncio.TimeoutError:
  pass
except asyncio.CancelledError:
  logger.warning('waiting for trades canceled')
  raise asyncio.CancelledError
except Exception as e:
  log.critical(str(e))
  raise e

ccxt.base.errors.ExchangeError: mexc {"code":700013,"msg":"Invalid content Type."}

```

It seems something is not right with the headers. Can somebody take a look?
issue_comments: Hello @dubisoft-solutions thanks a lot for letting us know we will take a look asap.

@dubisoft-solutions For some reason, I can't reproduce the issue; what's your Python version?
Would you mind sharing a verbose log? `exchange.verbose = True`

python version 3.12.1

It is working again, probably there is some underlying logic that in case of retries makes a request and misses the content-type header?

@carlosmiei , reproduced again This is the simple version of my code ``` exchange = ccxt.pro.mexc({ 'apiKey': '{key}', 'secret': '{secret}', }) symbol = 'ZEPH/USDT' print(await exchange.fetch_balance()) await exchange.watch_my_trades(symbol) ``` Run it and wait about 30 mins or so. I think something is happening internally, probably retrying logic

issue_title: Withdrawal request on MEXC returns "Signature for this request is not valid"
issue_body:
### Operating System

Linux

### Programming Languages

PHP

### CCXT Version

4.2.46

### Description

I am using the php version 4.2.46 of CCXT to connect to Mexc on a linux with php 8.2 and I can succesfully place orders, check orders, cancel orders, but can't withdraw (tried 5 different coins and different networks, USDT included) and I always get the same error "ccxt\AuthenticationError: mexc {"code":700002,"msg":"Signature for this request is not valid."}". I even checked the way the signature is created in the class and it's created the same way as per a place order request.

### Code

```
$exchange_to = '\\ccxt\\mecx';
$bot = new $exchange_to(array(
    'apiKey' => $api_key_public,
    'secret' => $api_key_private,
    'timeout' => 60000,

issue_title: Bitget error when use fetch_deposit_address
issue_body:
### Operating System

Windows 10 

### Programming Languages

Python

### CCXT Version

4.1.84

### Description

There is such a problem in bitget. I can't get the deposit address if there is a dollar sign in the ticker($AI, $ALT for example), I will get `{"code":"42017","msg":"The current chain does not support deposit the coin"` error. I removed the dollar using commonCurrency, but it doesn't help. I also noticed that commonCurrency does not work with bitget. For example, I included Toncoin there, but I will receive the following error - `code":"40019","msg":"Parameter coin cannot be empty`. I have not seen this with other exchanges. I also understand that if i put 'ALT':'ALT0', $ALT':'ALT', in commonCurrencies I always will got None as a result of my funcion but I dont know how to deal with it. 
  I also want to add, that after some test I think that this problem with obtaining a deposit address while currency in commonCurrencies occurs not only in bitget. For example, with gate and 'GTC'(Game.com in commonCurrencies) if we try exchange.fetchNetworkDepositAddress('GTC'), we will get  gate does not have currency code GTC, but if we try exchange.fetchNetworkDepositAddress('Game.com') we will get gate {"label":"INVALID_CURRENCY","message":"invalid currency"}. 
  With mexc for example if commonCurrency = {'GMT':'GOMINING, 'STEPN':'GMT',} if i use exchange.fetchDepositAddressesByNetwork('GMT') and exchange.fetchDepositAddressesByNetwork('GOMINING') I will get the same networks and addresses which are related only to STEPN GMT/ So if I use exchange.fetchDepositAddressesByNetwork('GOMINING') I will get wrong networks and sometimes addresses.
  I also admit that I did something wrong. In any case, it would be nice if you checked it out.
issue_comments:
thanks for report! 
I will check it out now.
after digging into the problem for some time, it seems the issue must be happening on exchange side. i've reported their api support, and i will update you once there is any further update about this
@ttodua Could you please tell me which exchanges this bug occurs with? I mentioned bitget, mexc and gate, but there are probably others

@AlwxDavydov 
I have found out where the ccxt had issue with regard of those spec symbols, and pushed a fix.
thanks for your observation, i'll gradually fix on other exchanges, if you can, provide the names of exchanges where you faced that issue (metnion also method, `fetchDepositAddress` or whichever)
thanks

@ttodua I'll write what I noticed.
  On the MEXC exchange, when cyclically adding a currency to the commonCurrencies, we combine two currencies into one and will only receive the one whose ticker is on the MEXC. It can be difficult to understand. Here's an example. commonCurrencies = {'GMT':'GOMINING','STEPN':'GMT',}. And if we try fetchDepositAddressesByNetwork we will get two identical options in any currency. And the information will relate to the ticker STEPN all the time and ignore GOMINING at all. This behavior can be misleading and cause errors. 
  With the Kucoin exchange. If the token is in commonCurrencies. The only way to get data, to use fetch_deposit_address, and fetchDepositAddressesByNetwork will not work for thease tokens.
  With Gate and Bitget, it is just impossible to fetch deposit data while token in commonCurrencies. 
  I would also like to note that on Binance, Bybit and Huobi everything works as expected. So you can do  commonCurrencies = {'TWT':'BTC','BTC':'TWT', And when you do fetchDepositAddressesByNetwork('BTC') you will get data about TWT and vice versa. So Id like to see same behavior with gate, kucoin and bitget. I also want to mencion that I only tried deposit functions and do not know will the bug work with withdrawal or other methods.

If you look at the [`parseWsOrder` method,](https://github.com/ccxt/ccxt/blob/1bdff9799c1fd4bd3643f135a6cdd74ae57131f0/ts/src/pro/bitget.ts#L1253) you can see that the response returned calls the method `safeOrder`. [Inside `safeOrder` you can see that remaining is calculated from `amount` and `filled`](https://github.com/ccxt/ccxt/blob/1bdff9799c1fd4bd3643f135a6cdd74ae57131f0/ts/src/base/Exchange.ts#L2616) when it cannot be explicitly read from the exchange response

------------

What issues are you experiencing?

'amount': 16.2,
   'filled': 202.3524,
 'remaining': -186.1524,

Amount is equal to notional, which is not right
filled is correct
remaining is amount - filled which is totally not right

issue_title: Kucoin fetchOpenOrders for hf accounts not working
issue_body:
### Operating System

### CCXT Version

_No response_

### Description

When I do 

`exchange.fetchOpenOrders("TLOS/USDT", undefined, undefined, {"hf":true}) `

it returns empty array, although there is open limit order.
I traced the problem to here in kucoin.js:

```
const responseData = this.safeValue(response, 'data', {});
const orders = this.safeValue(responseData, 'items', []);
```

it seems there is no 'items' key in the returned object for hf orders - it exists when fetching non hf orders.

issue_title: Cannot set timeout lower than 1 second
issue_body:
### Operating System

Linux

### Programming Languages

Python

### CCXT Version

4.1.50

### Description

If timeout is set to less than 1000 ms then any method call will crash with "ValueError: Attempted to set connect timeout to 0, but the timeout cannot be set to a value less than or equal to 0."
Example code to replicate this:
```
import ccxt
b = ccxt.binance()
b.timeout = 999
b.public_get_ping()
```
A fix would be to remove "int" from line 600 in https://github.com/ccxt/ccxt/blob/master/python/ccxt/base/exchange.py#L600
requests.Session.request can handle float values of the timeout parameter.

### Code

```
  

```

then update ccxt in your app, i'll be fixed

```
import ccxt.pro as ccxtpro
import asyncio

async def observe_trades(exchange, symbol):

    while True:
        try:
            trades = await exchange.watch_trades(symbol)
            # print(">trades")

        except ccxtpro.NetworkError as e:
            print(f"Network error in watch_trades: {e}")
        except ccxtpro.ExchangeError as e:
            print(f"Exchange error in watch_trades: {e}")
        except ccxtpro.BaseError as e:
            print(f"CCXT base error in watch_trades: {e}")
        except Exception as e:
            print(f"Other error in watch_trades: {e}")

async def observe_orderbooks(exchange, symbol):

    while True:
        try:
            orderbook = await exchange.watch_order_book(symbol)
            # print(">orderbook")
                
        except ccxtpro.NetworkError as e:
            print(f"Network error in watch_order_book: {e}")
        except ccxtpro.ExchangeError as e:
            print(f"Exchange error in watch_order_book: {e}")
        except ccxtpro.BaseError as e:
            print(f"CCXT base error in watch_order_book: {e}")
        except Exception as e:
            print(f"Other error in watch_order_book: {e}")

async def main():

    exchange_name = 'binance'

    exchange_class = getattr(ccxtpro, exchange_name)
    exchange = exchange_class()    
    markets = await exchange.load_markets()
    symbollist = [symbol for symbol in markets if symbol.endswith('/USDT:USDT')]

    tasks = []
    
    for symbol in symbollist:
        tasks.append(observe_trades(exchange, symbol))
        tasks.append(observe_orderbooks(exchange, symbol))

    await asyncio.gather(*tasks)


asyncio.run(main())
```

It's the same bug in this issue: https://github.com/ccxt/ccxt/issues/20412. 
I'm also waiting for the pr to be merged: https://github.com/ccxt/ccxt/pull/20563.

issue_title: Bingx watch_ohlcv can not set market type to "swap" successfully
issue_body:
### Operating System

not relevant

### Programming Languages

Python

### CCXT Version

4.1.97

### Description

Hi,

The watch_ohlcv function in pro/bingx.py does not provide the "time" value for "swap" in the response, but instead returns None. E.g.,
`[[None, 43725.2, 43894.2, 43719.8, 43831.0, 581.0491]].
`
The root cause is that Bingx sends the OHLCV time in the 'T' parameter for "swap", as opposed to the expected "t" used in "spot". This bug was addressed and fixed in the previous [issue](https://github.com/ccxt/ccxt/issues/20515) I reported.

However, for this fix to work, market['spot'] must be set to false (as seen in the fixed code), which is not the case in the current version.

As far as I can identify, this issue is caused by a mistake in the handle_ohlcv function in pro/bingx.py. The mistake is in the following line:
`marketType = client.url.find('swap') >= 'swap' if 0 else 'spot'
`
It should be corrected to:
`marketType = 'swap' if client.url.find('swap') >= 0 else 'spot'
`
This correction successfully sets market['spot'] to false and returns the correct value in watch_ohlcv:
`[[1703379600000, 43725.2, 43897.6, 43719.8, 43860.1, 916.6858]]
`

Besides the handle_ohlcv function, the exact same line containing the same mistake is also present in handle_order_book and handle_trades within pro/bingx.py. However, I have not tested its impact on these functions, so my explanation is specific to watch_ohlcv.

Thanks

### Code

```
import ccxt.pro
from asyncio import run

print('CCXT Pro version', ccxt.pro.__version__)

async def main():
    exchange = ccxt.pro.bingx({
        'newUpdates': False,
        'options': {
            'OHLCVLimit': 500,
            'defaultType': 'swap', # swap , spot, margin, future, delivery
        }
    })
    print(exchange.options)
    print(exchange.options['defaultType'])

    await exchange.load_markets()
    while True:
        try:
            ohlcvs = await exchange.watch_ohlcv(symbol='BTC/USDT:USDT', timeframe='1h', since=None, limit=None,params={})
            print(ohlcvs)
        except Exception as e:
            print(type(e).__name__, str(e))
            break
    await exchange.close()

run(main())
```
issue_comments:
Hey @kadiraltan thanks for pointing this out and finding the specific issue, the ternary operator we were using for `marketType` in TypeScript was a bit too complex to be handled and transpiled to python correctly. I opened a pull request that adjusts the implementation so that the python version will work as expected. The new python code will look like this:
```python
isSwap = client.url.find('swap') >= 0
marketType = 'swap' if isSwap else 'spot'
```

issue_comments:
Hi @hodlerhacks 

Can you set `newUpdates` to false (https://github.com/ccxt/ccxt/wiki/ccxt.pro.manual#newupdates-mode)? The default value is true, and you'll get the latest response from exchange server (it's single order from binance).

Hi @sc0Vu, thanks for your quick response.

Interestingly, this has never been an issue during the past 2.5 years. Also, not everyone has this problem with Binance. I cannot reproduce this myself. Has anything changed?

My application is used with many different exchanges. Can I apply this setting safely with any exchange, or could it affect the proper working? I'm a bit concerned about changing this, because in fact I do want to just receive the latest updates, so that would require newUpdates to be true.

Hi @hodlerhacks, I think the default value of newUpdates had been changed a couple of months ago.

If you set this value to flase, the return value would be array or orders, you'll need to loop orders for the updates.

With newUpdates, you only received the updated order (single object).

Hi @sc0Vu, I'm still a bit unsure what to do here. I do only want to receive the most recent updates. This is what's on the link that you shared:

_The result from the newUpdates mode will be one or more updates that have occurred since the last time exchange.watchMethod resolved. CCXT Pro can return one or more orders that were updated since the previous call._

And more:

_The result of calling exchange.watchOrders will look like shown below:_

```
[
    order, // see https://github.com/ccxt/ccxt/wiki/Manual#order-structure
    order,
    order,
    ...
]
```

As documented, an array should be expected as a result. That is exactly the behavior I've always observed, and it is what I want to use moving forward. So changing the setting to false doesn't seem like the way to go.

In addition to that, I'm quite sure there is something weird going on. For some installations, the result from watchOrders is an array, even if there is just one order. Like it has always been, for a wide range of exchanges. However, only some installations do not get an array back. I can't pinpoint what might be the root cause, but I do think there is a bug somewhere that is causing this.

Hi again @sc0Vu, analyzing a bit more with the affected users, it seems the problem appeared for them with the update to v4.1.72. Could the problem be related to https://github.com/ccxt/ccxt/pull/20163 by any chance?

                    origclientorderidlist = self.safe_value(extendedParams, 'origclientorderidlist', [])
                    extendedParams = self.omit(extendedParams, ['orderidlist', 'origclientorderidlist'])
                    query = self.rawencode(extendedParams)
                    orderidlistLength = len(orderidlist)
                    origclientorderidlistLength = len(orderidlist)  # This may be wrong
                    if orderidlistLength > 0:
                        query = query + '&' + 'orderidlist=[' + ','.join(orderidlist) + ']'
                    if origclientorderidlistLength > 0:
                        query = query + '&' + 'origclientorderidlist=[' + ','.join(origclientorderidlist) + ']'
```
the `origclientorderidlist` is added if `origclientorderidlistLength > 0`, but  `origclientorderidlistLength` is length of `orderidlist` instead of `origclientorderidlist`

### Code

```
  

```

issue_title: Websockets with Binance testnet
issue_body:
### Operating System

Windows with WSL2

### Programming Languages

Python

### CCXT Version

4.1.64

### Description

I'm trying to use the `watch_trades_for_symbols` API on Binance testnet and it doesn't appear to be working as expected. The following does not return any trade:

```
import ccxt.pro as ccxt

symbol = 'BTCUSDT' # or BTC/USDT:USDT

exchange = ccxt.binance({'options': {'defaultType': 'future', }})
exchange.set_sandbox_mode(True)

while True:
    trades = await exchange.watch_trades_for_symbols([symbol])
    print(trades)
```

while the following does:

```
import ccxt.pro as ccxt

symbol = 'BTCUSDT' # or BTC/USDT:USDT

exchange = ccxt.binanceusdm()
exchange.set_sandbox_mode(True)

while True:
    trades = await exchange.watch_trades_for_symbols([symbol])
    print(trades)
```

The strange thing is that the first one does return results in normal mode. Although, not sure if that one generally returns aggregated trades because IDs aren't the same as when using fetch_trades.

Watching positions like this does work but does not update new positions. It only reports once on the existing position but gets the right one.

```
import ccxt.pro as ccxt

symbol = 'BTCUSDT' # or BTC/USDT:USDT

exchange = ccxt.binance({'options': {'defaultType': 'future', }})
exchange.set_sandbox_mode(True)

while True:
    positions = await exchange.watch_positions([symbol])
    print(positions)
```

`watch_position_for_symbols` does not work and throws a `watch_positions` wasn't awaited error, though.

`watch_order_book_for_symbols` does work as well and for `watch_ohlcv_for_symbols` I'm not sure as I might have used wrong input. 

Is this expected?

### Code

```
  

```
issue_comments:
Hi @pbosch ,

Thanks for reporting, I opened a PR #20136 to fix the `watch_position_for_symbols` error.

For `watchTradesForSymbols` I opened PR #20137 to fix the issue

With respect to receiving new updates when calling watchPositions, do you receive an update if you modify your position?

Hi @pcriadoperez 

No, I just get the initial one and then nothing even when I open and close positions. I would expect to get an update each time it changes.

Something else I found is that `watch_orders_for_symbols` doesn't work either, it's apparently not implemented for binance? That was a bit surprising as the announcement mentioned binance directly. `watch_orders` works as expected.

Hi @pcriadoperez @carlosmiei 

I still encounter these issues in 4.1.67. As well as `watch_positions` only giving a result once and `watch_orders_for_symbols` not being implemented.

```js
const ccxt = require('ccxt')

const { api_key, secret } = require("bot/trading.env")

const TRADING_EXCHANGE = 'krakenfutures'
const TRADING_PAIR = 'BTC/USD:USD'
const OPEN_AMOUNT = 0.0005
const DELTA = 100

const main = async () => {
    console.log(ccxt.version)
    const exchange = new ccxt.pro[TRADING_EXCHANGE]({ apiKey: api_key, secret: secret, newUpdates: true })
    exchange.checkRequiredCredentials()
    monitorOrderStatus(exchange)
    const ticker = await exchange.fetchTicker(TRADING_PAIR)
    const price  = ticker.close - DELTA
    let order = await exchange.createOrder(TRADING_PAIR, 'limit', 'buy', OPEN_AMOUNT, price)

    await sleep(3000)

    exchange.verbose = true
    console.log('edit order price')
    const newPrice = order.price - DELTA
    order = await exchange.editOrder(order.id, order.symbol, order.type, order.side, order.remaining, newPrice)

    await sleep(3000)

    console.log('cancel order')
    order = await exchange.cancelOrder(order.id, TRADING_PAIR)
}

async function monitorOrderStatus(exchange) {
    if (exchange.has['watchOrders']) {
        while (true) {
            try {
                const orderStatus = await exchange.watchOrders(TRADING_PAIR)
                console.log(orderStatus)
            } catch (e) {
                console.log(e)
                // throw e
            }
        }
    }
}

async function sleep(ms) {
    return new Promise((resolve) => {
        setTimeout(resolve, ms)
    })
}

main()

macOS 14

JavaScript

### Description

I can place FOK or IOC order easily on binance, kucoin, gate but not on Huobi.

On market ETH/USDT
symbol= market.symbol

amount = 1
price = 1500

I tried different params, always as object :

issue_title: Bitget: Watch Stop Orders
issue_body:
### Operating System

Debian

### Programming Languages

JavaScript

### CCXT Version

4.0.103

### Description

I am connecting websocket to bring order status changes
`const orders = await bitget.watchOrders()
`
What happens on Bitget is that it separates the orders having the orders channel (limt/market orders) and ordersAlgo (stop orders)

I made a second call focusing on this pattern:
`const stopOrders = await bitget.watchOrders(undefined, undefined, undefined, { stop: true, sandboxMode: true })
`
Because I noticed that the main function has this syntax:
`async watchOrders(symbol = undefined, since = undefined, limit = undefined, params = {})
`
I'm using the sandbox trying to use SXRPSUDT_SUMCBL

It's bringing normal orders but not stop orders

The parameters passed into the function within async watchOrders appear to be correct:

**node_modules/ccxt/dist/cjs/src/pro/bitget.js: around line 800**
`const orders = await this.watchPrivate(messageHash, subscriptionHash, args, params);
`
```
{
     messageHash: 'order',
     subscriptionHash: 'order:trades',
     args: { instType: 'SUMCBL', channel: 'ordersAlgo', instId: 'default' },
     params: { sandboxMode: true }
}
```

But it is bringing other information from an opening market order that I placed and not a stop order

Is there something I'm doing wrong?
I debugged the outputs in the CCXT code and as far as I was able to follow the implementation I did, it was on the correct path, but I don't know if there is anything else I need to do that I didn't do.

### Code

```
  

```

I can't reproduce, would you like to paste verbose log?

issue_title: krakenfutures: private_post_batchorder()
issue_body:
### CCXT Version

4.1.22

### Description

Hello,

Regarding the documentation: 
https://docs.futures.kraken.com/#http-api-trading-v3-api-order-management-batch-order-management

I would like to use `krakenfutures.private_post_batchorder()` to cancel multiple orders.

Simple example in python to test:
```
exchange = ccxt.async_support.krakenfutures()
symbol = 'BTC/USD:USD'
orders = [await exchange.create_order(symbol, 'limit', 'buy', 0.0001, 27000), 
          await exchange.create_order(symbol, 'limit', 'buy', 0.0001, 27000)]
payload = {'batchOrder': []}
for order in orders:

await exchange.private_post_batchorder(payload)
```
raise

debug(with 'cliOrdId' in third parameter to test with all fields) :

issue_title: OKX - Wrong argument order for `self.handle_until_option`
issue_body:
### Operating System

_No response_

### Programming Languages

Python

### CCXT Version

4.1.6

### Description

Since 2 weeks ago one of the methods `fetch_my_trades` stopped working for `ccxt` and instead of returning the "swap" values, as specified in the `params` argument, it always returned "spot".

I did some checks and it seems that [this new addition](https://github.com/ccxt/ccxt/blame/9a1c7f741240edd443c6cfcab85757e5f445b6e9/python/ccxt/async_support/okx.py#L3708) is the culprit.
```python
request, params = self.handle_until_option('end', params, request)
```
The function itself takes `request` before `params`:
```python
    def handle_until_option(self, key, request, params, multiplier=1):
        until = self.safe_value_2(params, 'until', 'till')
        if until is not None:
            request[key] = self.parseToInt(until * multiplier)
            params = self.omit(params, ['until', 'till'])
        return [request, params]
```

 Swapping it on the local machine fixed the bug.


### Code

```
  

```
issue_comments: Hello @antipainK, thanks for reporting it and sorry for the inconvenience, we will fix it asap.
@antipainK Can you please update your ccxt version and try again? It should be working now.
Yes, the issue was resolved. Thanks for the fast response :)

issue_title: coinone order parsing error: string indices must be integers
issue_body:
### Operating System

macOS Sonoma 14.0

### Programming Languages

Python

### CCXT Version

4.1.14

### Description

The response data after calling the create_order API is as follows.
```
{
  "result": "success",
  "errorCode": "0",
  "orderId": "8a82c561-40b4-4cb3-9bc0-9ac9ffc1d63b"
}
```
An error occurs while processing this data in parse_order.

```
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.10/site-packages/ccxt/coinone.py", line 509, in create_order
    return self.parse_order(response, market)
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.10/site-packages/ccxt/coinone.py", line 607, in parse_order
    base = self.safe_currency_code(baseId, market['base'])
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.10/site-packages/ccxt/base/exchange.py", line 3793, in safe_currency_code
    return currency['code']
TypeError: string indices must be integers
```

I think that the first condition is executed in safe_currency and the str type currency is returned, and an error occurs when trying to access the code property in safe_currency_code.

coinone.py
```
def parse_order(self, order, market=None):
...
  id = self.safe_string(order, 'orderId')
  baseId = self.safe_string(order, 'baseCurrency')
  quoteId = self.safe_string(order, 'targetCurrency')
  base = self.safe_currency_code(baseId, market['base'])
  quote = self.safe_currency_code(quoteId, market['quote'])
```

exchange.py
```
def safe_currency_code(self, currencyId: Optional[str], currency: Optional[Any] = None):
    currency = self.safe_currency(currencyId, currency)
    return currency['code']

...

def safe_currency(self, currencyId: Optional[str], currency: Optional[Any] = None):
  if (currencyId is None) and (currency is not None):
      return currency
  if (self.currencies_by_id is not None) and (currencyId in self.currencies_by_id) and (self.currencies_by_id[currencyId] is not None):
      return self.currencies_by_id[currencyId]
  code = currencyId
  if currencyId is not None:
      code = self.common_currency_code(currencyId.upper())
  return {
      'id': currencyId,
      'code': code,
  }
```

### Code

_No response_
issue_comments:
Hello @devshjeon,
We will take a look asap, thanks for letting us know.

I confirmed that the transpiler is working.

thank you
@devshjeon Yeah both the Python and PHP versions derive from the Typescript file 🙂

issue_title: coinone private api call problem: Strings must be encoded before hashing
issue_body:
### Operating System

Mac Sonoma 14.0

### Programming Languages

Python

### CCXT Version

4.1.13

### Description

The following error occurs when calling the coinone private API.

[my code]
```
import ccxt

cex = ccxt.coinone({
    'apiKey': API_KEY,
    'secret': SECRET,
})
cex.fetch_balance()
```
[error]

```
...
  File "/.../.pyenv/versions/3.10.5/lib/python3.10/hmac.py", line 184, in new
    return HMAC(key, msg, digestmod)
  File "/.../.pyenv/versions/3.10.5/lib/python3.10/hmac.py", line 60, in __init__
    self._init_hmac(key, msg, digestmod)
  File "/.../.pyenv/versions/3.10.5/lib/python3.10/hmac.py", line 67, in _init_hmac
    self._hmac = _hashopenssl.hmac_new(key, msg, digestmod=digestmod)
TypeError: Strings must be encoded before hashing
```

When I checked the sign code, I found that an error occurred in the encoding part.

[coinone.py]
```
  def sign(self, path, api='public', method='GET', params={}, headers=None, body=None):
    request = self.implode_params(path, params)
    query = self.omit(params, self.extract_params(path))
    url = self.urls['api']['rest'] + '/'
    if api == 'public':
        url += request
        if query:
            url += '?' + self.urlencode(query)
    else:
        self.check_required_credentials()
        url += self.version + '/' + request
        nonce = str(self.nonce())
        json = self.json(self.extend({
            'access_token': self.apiKey,
            'nonce': nonce,
        }, params))
        payload = self.string_to_base64(json)
        body = payload
        secret = self.secret.upper()
        signature = self.hmac(payload, self.encode(secret), hashlib.sha512)
        headers = {
            'Content-Type': 'application/json',
            'X-COINONE-PAYLOAD': payload,
            'X-COINONE-SIGNATURE': signature,
        }
    return {'url': url, 'method': method, 'body': body, 'headers': headers}
```
While processing it as encoded data in self.hmac,
I think an error occurs because when calling self.string_to_base64(json), it is encoded and then decoded again.
So, when calling self.mac, I wrapped it in encode and called it, and the call was successful.

[modified code]
```
    def sign(self, path, api='public', method='GET', params={}, headers=None, body=None):
        request = self.implode_params(path, params)
        query = self.omit(params, self.extract_params(path))
        url = self.urls['api']['rest'] + '/'
        if api == 'public':
            url += request
            if query:
                url += '?' + self.urlencode(query)
        else:
            self.check_required_credentials()
            url += self.version + '/' + request
            nonce = str(self.nonce())
            json = self.json(self.extend({
                'access_token': self.apiKey,
                'nonce': nonce,
            }, params))
            payload = self.string_to_base64(json)
            body = payload
            secret = self.secret.upper()
            signature = self.hmac(self.encode(payload), self.encode(secret), hashlib.sha512)
            headers = {
                'Content-Type': 'application/json',
                'X-COINONE-PAYLOAD': payload,
                'X-COINONE-SIGNATURE': signature,
            }
        return {'url': url, 'method': method, 'body': body, 'headers': headers}
```

Please check the issue. thank you

### Code

_No response_

Hello @devshjeon,

issue_title: Coins.ph fetch_markets() returns zero active markets
issue_body:
### Operating System

Linux Mint

### Programming Languages

Python

### CCXT Version

4.1.4

### Description

`fetch_markets` returns markets where 'active' is always `False` despite exchange returning value 'trading' for 'status'. The reason is that line 550 of coinsph.py expect capital letters:
```python
            isActive = self.safe_string(market, 'status') == 'TRADING'

Coins.ph API documentation showcases small letters for this field, but that is evidently outdated:

exchange = ccxt.coinsph()

issue_title: Unable to watch_trades() with some markets
issue_body:
Hello,

I'm wondering why it's possible to `watch_trades` Bitget's markets like `BTC/USDT` and `BTC/USDT:USDT` with `ccxt.pro`, but not `BTC/USD:BTC`. A similar issue happens with Bitmart, market `BTC/USDT` can be monitored but not `BTC/USDT:USDT`. 

What could be the reason ? I'm not sure if it's CCXT Po limitation or if the class instance needs to be configured somehow.

Thank you,
Kinzowa
issue_comments:
This is the error I got for Bitget : 

`An error occurred with bitget BTC/USD:BTC: bitget {"event":"error","arg":{"instType":"mc","channel":"trade","instId":"BTCUSD_DMCBL"},"code":30001,"msg":"instType:mc,channel:trade,instId:BTCUSD_DMCBL doesn't exist","op":"subscribe"}, <class 'ccxt.base.errors.BadRequest'>`

For `BTC/USDT:USDT `market on Bitmart `fetch_trades` receives not message and generates no error.

ccxt==4.1.4

Thank you for letting us know. This was a bug related incorrect parsing of the websocket market ids for coin-margined and usdc-margined contracts. 

I pushed a [PR](https://github.com/ccxt/ccxt/pull/19472) that will fix this for all perpertual contracts. Delivery future contracts will still not be able to be watched however because bitget has not written implementation on their api yet.

issue_title: huobi withdraw problem with networks
issue_body:
### Operating System

Linux

### Programming Languages

JavaScript

### CCXT Version

latest

### Description

When I try to make withdraw of USDT ERC20 like this:
```
await exchange.withdraw ('USDT', 100, '0xxxxxxxx', undefined, {
    network: 'ERC20'
})
```
I see that 
```
RequestBody:
{"address":"0xxxxxx","currency":"usdt","chain":"ERC20","amount":100}
 ```
I get an error
` {"status":"error","err-code":"base-currency-chain-error","err-msg":"The current currency chain does not exist","data":null} `
So mapping of `chain` is incorrect. It was all good a few weeks ago, maybe some changes on Huobi.
The correct mapping of ERC20 network is `usdterc20` (but `trc20usd` for TRC20)
Can you please make some workaround of this issue?


### Code

_No response_
issue_comments: Hello @ndubel, thanks for letting us know we will take a look.

it's strange. 
what I see, we didn't have any update neither in huobi  class (`fetchCurrencies, withdraw, networkCodeToId` methods) neither in base related to this subject.
So, can you tell how much period could be "few weeks ago"? What I see, the last update for it was made around 8 months ago.
I am checking the issue again at this moment, and will keep you informed.

after digging into it, seems exchange changed response value in one of the fields of `v2/reference/currencies`.
I will be updating the implementation and migrating into another endpoint, as this endpoint become partially useless after that change. I will submit a fix within a few hours.

I've checked that my last succesful USDT withdrawal via API to ERC20 was made in the end of August. maybe ERC20 was default network so `chain` param was ignored or something like that. I also didn't made any changes in my code
thanks @ndubel again, sure, you were right, as I've checked, it has really been changed in endpoint response. I'm working on it still, upcoming PR will be merged  within 24 hours probably

issue_title: AttributeError: 'bybit' object has no attribute 'parseContractOrder'
issue_body:
### Operating System

Linux Ubuntu 20.04

### Programming Languages

Python

### CCXT Version

4.0.101 (4.0.100, 4.0.99)

### Description

Hi, I'm using ccxt (latest version) to place a limit order and the ccxt api watch_orders  to receive order update by websocket.
The watch_orders  fails to get order status and return this error:  AttributeError: 'bybit' object has no attribute 'parseContractOrder'
The strange thing is that days ago ( 2023/9/18 )  the problem did not appear.

            symbol,

                'stopLoss': stop_loss_price,

                #'positionIdx' : position_idx,
            },            
        )
                
        # Return the order object
        return order

    except ccxt.NetworkError as e:
        print(f"Network error: {e}")
    except ccxt.InsufficientFunds as e:
        print(f"InsufficientFunds error: {e}")
    except ccxt.ExchangeError as e:
        print(f"Exchange error: {e}")
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"An error occurred: {e}")

      
# Function to fetch order book data and make trading decisions with order book depth analysis
async def make_trading_decision(symbol):
  
    trade_amount = 100  # Replace with your fixed USDT amount
    take_profit_pct = 0.006  # Set your desired take profit percentage
    stop_loss_pct = 0.005  # Set your desired stop loss percentage    
    side = 'buy'
    
    try:

        # Fetch order book data
        order_book = await exchange.fetch_order_book(symbol)

        best_bid = order_book['bids'][0][0]
        best_ask = order_book['asks'][0][0]

        # Calculate the spread threshold as a percentage of the current price
        entry_price = best_bid 
        quantity = trade_amount / entry_price
        
        # Calculate take profit and stop loss prices
        take_profit_price = entry_price * (1 + take_profit_pct)
        stop_loss_price = entry_price * (1 - stop_loss_pct)

        # Place the limit order with take profit and stop loss
        order = await place_order(symbol, side, entry_price, quantity, take_profit_price, stop_loss_price)
        print(f"{side.capitalize()} trade entered for {symbol} at {entry_price}, Quantity: {quantity:.4f} {symbol.split('/')[0]}")
        print(f"Take Profit: {take_profit_price}, Stop Loss: {stop_loss_price}")

    except ccxt.NetworkError as e:
        print(f"Network error: {e}")
    except ccxt.ExchangeError as e:
        print(f"Exchange error: {e}")
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"An error occurred: {e}")      
      
# Function to monitor orders and watch their trades asynchronously
async def monitor_orders():    
    while True:
        try:
          orders = await exchange.watch_orders()
                     
          for order in orders:
                print(f'receive trade notify: {order}')

        except ccxt.NetworkError as e:
            print(f"Network error: {e}")
            #return {}
        except ccxt.ExchangeError as e:
            print(f"Exchange error: {e}")
            #return {}
        except Exception as e:
            print(f"An error occurred: {e}")
            #return {}
    
        
async def main():
  # Start the order monitoring function concurrently
  asyncio.create_task(monitor_orders())    

  await make_trading_decision('BTC/USDT:USDT')

  # Initialize the list of symbols to trade by fetching available tokens
  while True:

      # strategy loop

      # Add a delay between trading decisions (adjust as needed)
      await asyncio.sleep(10)
      

asyncio.get_event_loop().run_until_complete(main())

issue_title: Bitfinex2 - fetch_ledger mislabeling ledger entries (for deposit/withdrawal)
issue_body:
### Operating System

Ubuntu 22.04.2 LTS

### Programming Languages

Python

### CCXT Version

4.0.56

### Description

Bitfinex2 fetch_ledger is mislabeling the ledger entries (at least for deposits) because of the conditional logic in parse_ledger_entry_type()

A deposit entry from bitfinex (description) will look like this:

`'Deposit (BITCOIN) #INT_HERE on wallet exchange'`

The logic for labeling these goes like this:

```
if type is None:
    return None
elif type.find('fee') >= 0 or type.find('charged') >= 0:
    return 'fee'
elif type.find('exchange') >= 0 or type.find('position') >= 0:
    return 'trade'
elif type.find('rebate') >= 0:
    return 'rebate'
elif type.find('deposit') >= 0 or type.find('withdrawal') >= 0:
    return 'transaction'
elif type.find('transfer') >= 0:
    return 'transfer'
elif type.find('payment') >= 0:
    return 'payout'
else:
    return type
```

So because 'exchange' exists in the description string, and because that conditional block is evaluated before the deposit or withdrawal conditional check, it is being labeled as a trade instead of a transaction

### Code

```
  

```
issue_comments: Hello @ryanmccoy, thanks for letting us know, we will change the order of the conditions.

T-T - https://www.huobi.com/en-us/opend/newApiPages/?id=8cb77159-77b5-11ed-9966-0242ac110003 The following is the error message: ``` ccxt.base.errors.InvalidOrder: huobi {"status":"error","err_code":1067,"err_msg":"The client_order_id field is invalid. Please re-enter.","ts":1694589259929} ``` I think there's something wrong with this part of the code: ### Code ```Python huobi = ccxt.huobi() huobi.options['defaultType'] = 'swap' huobi.options['marginMode'] = 'cross' huobi.create_order(..., params={'client_order_id': 123123123, ....}) ```
